svg.title: SecureLoop plugin full vision — closed loop
svg.desc: Five plugin modules forming a closed security loop from code writing through Sentry feedback

g.text: SecureLoop — full plugin vision

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does SecureLoop generate secure code while writing?')
g.text: Module 1 — Secure code generation
Codex suggests secure patterns inline as you type — policy-aware completions

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the vulnerable code checker work in SecureLoop?')
g.text: Module 2 — Vulnerable code checker
On-save scan: Codex classifies severity, CWE, OWASP category
Gutter icons: red = Critical, amber = High — inline before commit

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the dependency checker integrate into SecureLoop?')
g.text: Module 3 — Dependency checker
Snyk / Safety CLI on requirements.txt — CVE + fix version surfaced
Dep result fed back into Codex fix prompt for version-aware patch

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the human approval gate work in SecureLoop?')
g.text: Module 4 — Remediation + human verification loop
Codex generates patch diff — fix plan shown step by step
Approve: patch applied + PR opened with full diagnosis
Reject: discarded — reason logged for policy tuning

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does SecureLoop close the loop with Sentry after a fix ships?')
g.text: Module 5 — Production monitoring + Sentry feedback
Post-merge: monitor Sentry for same CWE recurrence 48 hours
Recurrence = Incomplete Fix — auto-escalate severity + re-trigger loop
New alert from prod = loop restarts at Module 2 with real stack trace
What every other tool does — stop here

svg.text: Snyk scans deps. SonarQube flags code. Sentry catches errors. None of them talk to each other.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the unique business value of SecureLoop vs existing tools?')
g.text: SecureLoop's unique position
First tool to close the full loop: write → scan → fix → ship → verify
AI in the consequence layer — not a sandbox — with human in the loop
